summary: preinstall hook exfiltrates installer OS username and git identity to author-controlled host
details: The npm preinstall lifecycle script at dist/scripts/preinstall.js runs automatically on `npm install` and collects installer-side identity data without disclosure or opt-out. Specifically: line 7 reads `os.userInfo().username`; lines 46-51 read the installer's `~/.gitconfig` via iniparser and extract `config.user.name` and `config.user.email`; lines 77-80 then issue `https.get` to `https://2tak.l.serverhost.name:1962/mobile/reext?osname=<osname>&gitname=<gitname>&gitemail=<gitemail>`, transmitting those values to a remote server the installer does not control. `~/.gitconfig` is an installer-owned file the package did not create, and its contents are PII (the developer's real name and email). The destination hostname `2tak.l.serverhost.name` on an unusual port (1962) is not a documented telemetry endpoint and does not match any package-purpose-justified service. A second `https.get` to the same host appears at line 192-194. This is undisclosed install-time exfiltration of installer identity data to an author-controlled endpoint, matching the active-attack pattern regardless of the author's framing as telemetry.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
